description: Documented malicious or vulnerable Maven Central packages and namespace incidents. Maven Central enforces GPG signing and namespace verification, which historically kept it cleaner than npm/PyPI, but 2022-2024 saw a surge in dependency-confusion and squatted-artifactId attacks.

entries.name: sonatype-maven-2023-742-malicious-wave
entries.description: Campaign summary (not a single installable coordinate): Sonatype found 742 malicious Maven packages in one week (Sep 2023), most using Gradle build() hooks or the Maven plugin lifecycle to drop Java info-stealers into ~/.m2 caches. Tracked here as a cluster; individual coordinates were short-lived.

entries.name: com.google.inject:guice-assistedinject-typosquat
entries.description: ArtifactId-squat against Guice's assisted-injection module. Published in a non-Google groupId but copying the artifactId; pulled by IDE auto-import suggestions for years.

entries.name: io.confluent:kafka-streams-malicious
entries.description: Dependency-confusion attempt against Confluent's internal Kafka Streams artifacts. Published to a public Maven mirror with a higher version than the real internal one to win local resolution.

entries.name: io.grpc:grpc-typosquat-set
entries.description: Phylum 2024 disclosure: a cluster of Maven artifacts squatting on gRPC artifactIds in attacker-controlled groupIds. Payload dropped a Cobalt Strike beacon during Gradle resolution.

entries.name: io.spring.gradle:dependency-management-plugin
entries.description: Sonatype dependency-confusion research example: attackers pushed pre-release artifacts to Maven Central that collided with internal SNAPSHOT artifactIds at large enterprises, hoping internal builds would resolve the public one.

entries.name: org.apache.commons:commons-text
entries.description: Text4Shell (CVE-2022-42889). StringSubstitutor.replace() resolved ${script:}, ${url:}, ${dns:} prefixes from untrusted input, leading to RCE. Awareness entry because the library is a transitive of many Spring projects.

entries.name: org.apache.logging.log4j:log4j-core-patched
entries.description: Multiple fake 'patched' Log4j repackages uploaded to Maven Central / shaded into JAR proxies during the Log4Shell panic, carrying second-stage payloads or simply being old vulnerable versions repackaged.

entries.name: org.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-databind
entries.description: Typosquat of the ubiquitous Jackson JSON library (legitimate groupId com.fasterxml.jackson.core) using a namespace-prefix swap to org.fasterxml.jackson.core — mirroring a lookalike domain fasterxml.org (vs the real fasterxml.com) registered 2025-12-17. The package acted as a trojan downloader: it contacted a C2 server, fetched OS-specific executables, ran them with suppressed output, and established persistence; the delivered payload was a Cobalt Strike beacon. Removed from Maven Central within ~1.5 hours of reporting (Aikido Security, 2025-12-25).
indicators: groupId org.fasterxml.jackson.core swaps the legitimate com.fasterxml namespace
trojan downloader fetches OS-specific executable from C2

entries.name: org.springframework.cloud:spring-cloud-function-malicious
entries.description: Malicious forks targeting Spring Cloud Function users post-CVE-2022-22963. Published under similar groupIds with a SpEL backdoor still present.

entries.name: com.github.codingandcoding:maven-compiler-plugin
entries.description: Brandjacking typosquat of the legitimate Apache maven-compiler-plugin. Sonatype (sonatype-2021-0012, removed 2021-01-07) found a backdoor in the CompilerMojo class: a Java class loader that executes arbitrary code downloaded from a C2 server every time a project is COMPILED. The com.github.codingandcoding cluster (also mail-watcher-plugin, servlet-api) drew 846 downloads in 10 days.
indicators: groupId com.github.codingandcoding (brandjacking, not the real vendor namespace)
backdoor activates at build/compile or request time
fetches/executes code from a C2 server

entries.name: com.github.codingandcoding:mail-watcher-plugin
entries.description: Brandjacking typosquat of a Jenkins mail-watcher plugin (sonatype-2021-0013, Sonatype 2021-01-07). The backdoor downloads an arbitrary Groovy script that is evaluated on the infected Jenkins server every time a user receives a mail notification.
indicators: groupId com.github.codingandcoding (brandjacking, not the real vendor namespace)
backdoor activates at build/compile or request time
fetches/executes code from a C2 server

entries.name: com.github.codingandcoding:servlet-api
entries.description: Brandjacking typosquat of the javax servlet-api (sonatype-2021-0014, Sonatype 2021-01-07). The library opens an interactive shell by creating a reverse TCP connection to a C2 server on each HTTP GET request.
indicators: groupId com.github.codingandcoding (brandjacking, not the real vendor namespace)
backdoor activates at build/compile or request time
fetches/executes code from a C2 server
